overrides.override_summary: Post-term covenant not to compete may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may have more freedom to compete after leaving the system

overrides.override_summary: Interest rate capped at higher of 10% annually or 5% plus Federal Reserve Bank rate
overrides.why_it_matters: 1.5%/month (18%/year) in FA may be reduced for CA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Release cannot waive rights under CA Franchise Investment Law or CA Franchise Relations Act
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees retain franchise law rights even when signing releases on renewal/transfer

overrides.override_summary: FPR does not reflect costs/expenses — explicit warning required for CA prospective franchisees
overrides.why_it_matters: CA law requires clearer disclosure that FPR is revenue-only

overrides.override_summary: If franchisor fails to deliver products/services needed for substantial operation within 45 days, franchisee may demand cancellation
overrides.why_it_matters: CT franchisees have a 45-day delivery-or-cancel right

overrides.override_summary: No waiver of claims under applicable state franchise law, including fraud in the inducement
overrides.why_it_matters: HI franchisees cannot waive franchise law claims via questionnaires or acknowledgments

overrides.override_summary: Any provision designating jurisdiction/venue outside Illinois is void. Arbitration may still be outside IL.
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees can litigate in Illinois, not Delaware

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs the Franchise Agreement
overrides.why_it_matters: Delaware choice of law overridden for IL franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Any provision designating jurisdiction/venue outside Indiana is deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisees cannot be forced to litigate in Delaware

overrides.override_summary: Indiana law supersedes conflicting provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: Delaware law overridden where it conflicts with IN law

overrides.override_summary: Unilateral termination without good cause or in bad faith prohibited (IC 23-2-2.7-1(7))
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisees have stronger termination protections than the FA alone provides

overrides.override_summary: No release shall relieve franchisor from liability under Indiana franchise laws
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisees retain state law rights despite releases

overrides.override_summary: Release on transfer/renewal/assignment does not apply to claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees retain MD franchise law claims through releases

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring lawsuit in Maryland for MD franchise law claims
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees need not travel to Delaware for state franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Claims under MD franchise law must be commenced within 3 years of franchise grant
overrides.why_it_matters: 3-year statute of limitations for MD franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Surety bond required by MD Securities Commissioner until all franchisees receive training and are open for business
overrides.why_it_matters: MD provides additional financial protection for franchisees during startup

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination (60 days to cure), 180 days notice of non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees get substantially longer notice/cure periods than FA provides

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor prohibited from requiring general release (Rule 2860.4400(D))
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees cannot be required to sign releases on renewal or transfer

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota, cannot require jury waiver, cannot require liquidated damages or termination penalties
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees have full access to MN courts with jury rights

overrides.override_summary: All rights and causes of action under Article 33 of NY General Business Law remain in force (non-waiver provisions)
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchisees cannot waive NY franchise law protections

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration site must be agreeable to all parties, not remote from franchisee. Forum changed to North Dakota.
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees need not travel to Michigan for arbitration or Delaware for litigation

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law supersedes conflicting provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: Delaware law overridden where it conflicts with ND law

overrides.override_summary: Requiring general release on renewal is unfair and deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees cannot be required to sign releases on renewal

overrides.override_summary: Waiver of exemplary/punitive damages, jury trial, and liquidated damages are unfair and deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees retain rights to punitive damages and jury trial

overrides.override_summary: Covenants restricting competition are generally unenforceable under ND Century Code §9-08-06
overrides.why_it_matters: Post-term noncompete likely unenforceable for ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in Rhode Island for RI Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees need not travel to Delaware for state franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause per VA Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564
overrides.why_it_matters: VA franchisees have statutory protection against unreasonable termination

overrides.override_summary: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails on conflict
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchise law trumps FA provisions

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in WA or mutually agreed location. Litigation may be brought in WA.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees need not travel to Michigan/Delaware

overrides.override_summary: Release void except when executed in negotiated settlement with independent counsel per RCW 19.100.220(2)
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees cannot be forced to sign boilerplate releases on renewal/transfer

overrides.override_summary: Waiver of exemplary/punitive damages void. Treble damages available under RCW 19.100.190.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees can seek treble damages for franchise law violations

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void unless employee earnings exceed $100K/year or contractor earnings exceed $250K/year (adjusted for inflation)
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees and their employees may be exempt from noncompete depending on earnings

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate under any grounds permitted by state law. Buy-back without consent unlawful except for good cause termination.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees have broader termination rights and protection from forced buy-back
